name: hmac-signer
description: Produce an HMAC-SHA256 signature of input data using a secret key from the vault. Useful for webhook signature verification or request signing. Set SECRET_NAME config key to the vault path where your HMAC signing key is stored (canonical paths: 'webhooks/signing_secret' for generic webhooks, 'github/webhook_secret' for GitHub, 'stripe/webhook_secret' for Stripe).
setup_instructions: 1. Store your HMAC signing key at a canonical vault path:
2. Install with the specific path you used: install_module_from_catalog {name: 'hmac-signer', allowed_secrets: ['webhooks/signing_secret']}
3. In node config, set SECRET_NAME to the same vault path, and DATA_FIELD to the input field to sign
Note: Specify the exact vault path you used in both allowed_secrets and SECRET_NAME — they must match.

SECRET_NAME.description: Vault path for the HMAC signing key. Canonical paths: 'webhooks/signing_secret' (generic), 'github/webhook_secret' (GitHub), 'stripe/webhook_secret' (Stripe). Must exactly match the path used when the secret was stored and when the module was installed with allowed_secrets.

DATA_FIELD.description: Field in the input JSON containing the string to sign

INCLUDE_PREFIX.description: Optional prefix prepended to the signature (e.g., 'sha256=' for GitHub webhook style)
